summary: Business Ideas Advisor + Shopping & Deals Engine tested end-to-end. Backend: 15/16 new tests PASSED via public URL (test_business_shopping.py); regression on prior modules (Global/Security/Local/Investments) = 80/80 PASS, no regressions. Frontend: login, More→Business hub (2 tiles), Ideas Advisor (5 cards render + expand next steps), Eden Heights (hero/ROI/phases/checklist all visible with 4 ha / $12,000 / Year 4 breakeven, 8 checklist items, 3 phase rows, toggle works, edit municipality 'Sta. Maria' persists via PUT and re-renders), Shopping hub (3 tiles + savings card), Deals (5 cards), Utilities (4 cards), Registered Products (add Test RAV4 succeeds, card renders) — ALL VERIFIED. TWO CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND: (1) Cloudflare proxy 502/timeout at 60s — POST /api/business/ideas/{id}/plan internally takes ~235s (returns valid 33KB plan); the public URL returns 502 'Bad Gateway' after ~60s. Same risk applies to POST /api/shopping/utilities/{id}/find-better (Claude call, similar duration). Users hitting 'View Full Business Plan' or 'Find Better Rate' will see a failure modal. (2) React Native Alert.alert on react-native-web does NOT fire onPress callbacks for action buttons — Dismiss Deal and Delete Product flows are broken in the web preview (verified: deals count 4→4, products count 1→1 after action). They will work on native iOS/Android via Expo Go but block testing AND web users.

critical.issue: Endpoint works (returns ~33KB markdown in ~235s internally) but Cloudflare proxy 502s at 60s. Need streaming response, async job + polling, or shorter Claude prompt.
Same Claude-call pattern; same 60s Cloudflare timeout risk. Not stress-tested via public URL because it would just time out — but matches the failing pattern.

ui_bugs.issue: Uses Alert.alert with onPress callback for Dismiss button. react-native-web's Alert.alert only renders window.alert (no button callbacks fire), so dismissing a deal does nothing on web. Use Modal or a web-friendly confirm (Platform.OS === 'web' ? window.confirm(...) : Alert.alert(...)).
Same Alert.alert pattern in removeItem(). Delete trash icon does nothing on web preview. Same fix needed.

integration_issues.flow: Business Ideas Advisor → View Full Business Plan
integration_issues.issue: Frontend openPlan() awaits buildPlan() with no timeout/abort. Because Cloudflare returns 502 at 60s while Claude still runs ~3-4min, the modal shows 'Could not generate plan. Please retry.' Consider: (a) async-job pattern (POST returns job_id, GET polls), (b) reduce plan length / set max_tokens lower, or (c) stream tokens.
Same risk as plan endpoint — Claude call likely >60s.

react_native_issues.issue: react-native-web Alert.alert button callbacks never fire; breaks dismiss-deal + delete-product. Reference: /app/frontend/app/shopping/deals.tsx:70-85 and /app/frontend/app/shopping/products.tsx:60-73. Use Platform.OS check or replace with a custom Modal-based confirm.

action_items: FIX (frontend) shopping/deals.tsx dismiss handler — replace Alert.alert with Platform-aware confirm so onPress fires on web. Same for shopping/products.tsx removeItem.

FIX (backend or frontend) Claude-call timeout: either reduce Claude prompt/max_tokens for /business/ideas/{id}/plan and /shopping/utilities/{id}/find-better, or switch to a job-id async pattern with polling. Current ~235s response time is incompatible with the 60s Cloudflare proxy timeout for users on the public URL.
Consider showing a clearer error message in the plan modal when the request times out (currently 'Could not generate plan. Please retry.' — but retrying will time out again).
Optional: split server.py (now 4735 lines) into per-module routers.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 4735 lines — adding business+shopping made the file even larger. Strongly recommend splitting into /app/backend/routers/{business,shopping,...}.py.
DRY: SEED_BUSINESS_IDEAS, DEFAULT_EDEN_HEIGHTS, SEED_DEALS, SEED_UTILITIES inline in server.py (~100 lines) — move to /app/backend/seed/*.py.

/api/business/ideas/{idea_id}/plan and /api/shopping/utilities/{id}/find-better do synchronous Claude calls without timeout protection — should adopt async job + GET-poll pattern (already needed because Cloudflare proxy = 60s).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_business_shopping.py (NEW — 16 tests covering all Business + Shopping endpoints incl. seed verification, Claude generate-ideas live call, Eden Heights GET/PUT round-trip with persistence verification, deals total_savings≈$130 + dismiss flow, prefs GET/PUT, utilities listing, find-better live Claude call, registered-products full CRUD, name-required 400, and auth guards)

success_rate.backend: 94% (15/16 new — only failure is /business/ideas/{id}/plan via public URL hitting Cloudflare 60s timeout; endpoint itself works internally) + 100% regression (80/80 on prior modules)
success_rate.frontend: 78% (login, navigation, business hub, ideas list+expand, Eden Heights full screen incl. edit/persist, shopping hub, deals listing, utilities listing, products add+render all work; deal-dismiss + product-delete broken on web due to Alert.alert; plan/find-better Claude flows untestable via web due to Cloudflare timeout)

seed_data_creation: Cleared and re-verified seed data for business_ideas, eden_heights, dismissed_deals, shopping_prefs, registered_products collections via the new test. Seeds verified: 3 business ideas (Educational Consulting / Intl Financial / Eden Heights), default Eden Heights (4 ha / $12,000 / Bulacan / 3 phases / 6-point ROI / 8 checklist items), 5 deals (~$130 total savings), 4 utilities (Georgia Power, AT&T Wireless, AT&T Internet, DeKalb County Water), default shopping prefs (gas_threshold $2.85).
context_for_next_testing_agent: New endpoints all under /api/business/* and /api/shopping/*. Two known limitations on the public URL: (1) Claude endpoints exceeding 60s fail with Cloudflare 502, (2) RN Alert.alert callbacks don't fire on react-native-web so any Alert-confirmed action button (deal dismiss, product delete) is dead on web. test_business_shopping.py auto-clears collections before each test class so it's safe to re-run. testIDs to know: hub-ideas, hub-eden, ideas-back, idea-{i}, expand-{i}, plan-{i}, plan-close, generate-ideas, eden-back, eden-edit, eden-hero, eden-roi-chart, eden-phases, eden-checklist, phase-p1..p3, check-0..7, edit-municipality, edit-value, edit-save, edit-close, hub-deals, hub-utilities, hub-products, shopping-savings-card, deals-savings, deal-d1..d5, dismiss-d1..d5, utility-ga_power|att_wireless|att_internet|dekalb_water, find-{id}, reco-close, products-add, product-{id}, del-{id}, f-name, f-brand, f-model, cat-{electronics|appliance|auto|baby|food|other}, f-save, add-close. Tab bar items in (tabs)/_layout.tsx don't have testIDs — use get_by_text('More', exact=True).last.
rca_of_issue: Bug 1 (Cloudflare 502 on plan): /api/business/ideas/{idea_id}/plan internally completes in ~235s returning a valid 33KB markdown plan, but Cloudflare's preview proxy enforces a ~60s timeout, returning HTML 502 (Ray ID logged: a11ead977d3035c0). Reproduction: TOKEN=$(curl ... /api/auth/login); curl -X POST {PUBLIC_URL}/api/business/ideas/seed-1/plan -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' → 502 in ~60s. Same curl against localhost:8001 → 200 + 33KB plan in ~235s. Mitigation options: (a) Reduce Claude max_tokens to keep response <60s, (b) implement job-id/polling pattern (POST starts task → return job_id immediately; GET /api/business/plans/{job_id} polls status/result), (c) stream tokens. Frontend openPlan() in ideas.tsx swallows the failure into a generic message — please surface the timeout reason. Bug 2 (Alert.alert on web): React Native's Alert.alert on react-native-web does not present button callbacks — it falls back to window.alert which only shows title/message. The onPress arrow function inside the Dismiss/Remove button objects is never invoked on web. Reproduction: open /shopping/deals on web, tap dismiss icon — no dialog, no state change, no /dismiss POST. Fix: use Platform-aware confirm: `if (Platform.OS === 'web') { if (window.confirm('Dismiss this deal?')) { await shoppingApi.dismissDeal(id); ... } } else { Alert.alert(...) }`. Affected files: /app/frontend/app/shopping/deals.tsx:70-85, /app/frontend/app/shopping/products.tsx:60-73.
